Root cause: the agent computed action_count and the avg_datatypes_per_action denominator over ALL Actions on a domain, including Actions whose distinct data_type set is empty; the reference excludes empty-data_type Actions. what_required: Implement three zero-sum equilibrium solvers and write one results.json. Tier 3 required a 4-rank Leduc Hold'em MCCFR solver whose average strategy (504 info sets) is low-exploitability AND whose reported exploitability matches the evaluator's independent best-response recompute to within 0.02 — which demands the agent's Leduc game model (betting dynamics, pot accounting, terminal payoffs) exactly match the canonical specification.
what_happened: Tiers 1 and 2 passed; Tier 3 failed on exactly one of eight conditions: reported_exploitability_matches. The agent's Tier-3 output was otherwise schema-perfect (504 info sets, keys matching the evaluator 1:1 with zero key/length mismatches, correct [fold,call,raise] ordering, valid probs, exact game_params). It reported exploitability 0.0428, but the evaluator's independent best-response recompute returned -0.0502 (|diff|=0.093>0.02). A negative exploitability is impossible for a true BR in a shared game tree (decomposition: br0=-0.151 < br1_p0=-0.050), and no 3-action permutation fixes it — proving the MCCFR was trained on a Leduc payoff/betting model that subtly diverges from the grader's canonical one.
capability_named: Correct implementation of Leduc Hold'em extensive-form game dynamics (terminal payoff / pot / betting semantics) such that MCCFR converges to a strategy consistent with the canonical game model
evidence: scripts/score_outputs.py:594-660 (_score_tier3) shows the only failing condition is reported_exploitability_matches; scorer rerun gave details.tier3.exploitability=-0.0502 vs reported 0.0428. Decomposition via LeducBestResponse._compute_br: br0=-0.151, br1_p0=-0.050 (negative exploitability = game-model inconsistency). Permutation test: identity ordering -0.0502, all other 3-action perms 0.26-6.08. Info-set key/action-length comparison: 0 agent-only, 0 evaluator-only, 0 length mismatches. trajectory.json steps 7/40/48 show the agent knew the evaluator independently validates exploitability but never reconciled its game model.

The divergence traces to fit-level details: the model injected custom lmeControl (opt='optim', niter=50, custom tolerances) overriding glmmPQL defaults, which changes the converged coefficients (and thus exp(beta3)=ratio_week6 directly), plus sensitivity to spline knot placement (turn 26 shows fresh-vs-full basis swings week2 from 1.033 to 0.937). what_required: Read Table 2 chemical drawings from a PDF and reconstruct correct full-molecule SMILES for 17 ligands with matching EC50 values.
what_happened: The model produced a structurally valid CSV (correct headers, 17 rows, matching ligand-ID set, all SMILES RDKit-parseable) so no hard gate fired, but scored 0/17 on content. It guessed a single core scaffold and appended per-row R1 fragments it tried to read from the structure drawings; the core is chemically wrong (so every full-molecule InChIKey mismatches) and it could not distinguish the fused-ring R1 systems — producing byte-identical SMILES for distinct compounds (rows 31==35; 37==39==40) that have different potencies. what_required: Adjudicate, for 9 chest X-ray cases, which of two heavily-overlapping candidate bounding boxes (reader_a vs reader_b) better matches a subtle atelectasis opacity, then emit three TSVs; the boxes and log files each pass only if the selected_reader matches the hidden gold for ALL 9 cases (and box IoU>=0.5), while the impressions file is a fixed label vocabulary.
what_happened: The model executed the workflow correctly: read rules/manifest/clinical notes/both reader TSVs, rendered each DICOM to PNG with red (reader_a) / cyan (reader_b) box overlays via pydicom, used analyze_image vision to compare them, launched MicroDicom in the GUI (turns 63-74), and wrote all three TSVs with exact-correct schemas and constants. It produced genuine mixed selections (3 reader_a, 6 reader_b), not the all-reader_b fixture. It still scored 0.333: only final_impressions.tsv (deterministic labels) passed; adjudicated_boxes.tsv and adjudication_log.tsv both failed because its perceptual judgment of which near-identical box better fit the opacity disagreed with the hidden gold on at least one of the 9 cases, and each file is all-or-nothing. Some analyze_image calls returned empty strings, but the model retried and got usable vision results for the cases.
capability_named: fine-grained radiographic perceptual discrimination — judging which of two nearly-overlapping bounding boxes better localizes a subtle atelectasis opacity on a chest X-ray (radiologist-level box adjudication)
evidence: Output adjudicated_boxes.tsv has correct schema and mixed readers (e.g. 00009285_000=reader_a, 00013118_008=reader_b). eval_result.json score=0.3333. main.py _boxes_pass()/log check require selected_reader==gold for every case_id (all-or-nothing). analyze_image outputs were hedged perceptual calls like 'reader_b (CYAN) slightly better centered ... if uncertain choose the one whose center better aligns' (turns 47/49/51) — fine offsets like x 353 vs 348, y 532 vs 540 between the two candidate boxes.

what_required: Repair U4 in the KiCad GUI and emit grader-exact release artifacts.
what_happened: The agent (222 turns, ended cleanly with "DONE") correctly performed the core repair: U4 is assigned to nav_local_footprints:PCA9554_PW_local in both schematic and PCB, all deliverables are present and non-empty, so every hard gate passed. It lost exactly 0.40 on three quality checks: (1) it exported the netlist as `--format kicadxml` (XML), but the grader's `_netlist_u4_pin_nets` only parses s-expression netlists, so the agent's project.net parses to empty and can never match the reference (-0.20); (2,3) ERC and DRC report signatures didn't match the gold baseline — drc.json shows schematic_parity=18 residual Datasheet field mismatches (PCB '~' vs schematic ''), indicating the PCB-from-schematic update wasn't perfectly clean (-0.20).
capability_named: Producing grader-exact release artifacts: choosing KiCad's default s-expression netlist format (not kicadxml) and driving the PCB-from-schematic sync cleanly enough that ERC/DRC violation signatures match the gold reference.
